title: Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
text:
Matched Retirement Savings Scheme - SupportGoWhere
Matched Retirement Savings Scheme
Share link
Helps eligible Singaporeans with lower retirement savings to save more by matching cash top-ups made to their Retirement or Special Accounts (RA/SA). This will boost their monthly payouts in retirement.
SCHEME HIGHLIGHTS
Dollar-for-dollar matching grant on cash top-ups to your RA/SA of up to $2,000 per year
Boost CPF retirement savings and monthly payouts 
Earn CPF interest rates of up to 6% per annum on cash top-ups
Collapse all sections
About this support

The Government will match every dollar of cash top-ups made to the RA/SA of eligible Singaporeans of up to $2,000 per year, with a $20,000 cap over an eligible member’s lifetime

The matching grant will automatically be credited to the RA/SA of the eligible member at the beginning of the following year

Eligible Singaporeans will enjoy interest rates of up to 6% per annum on the cash top-up and matching grant, enhancing their retirement payouts

Cash top-ups that attract the MRSS matching grant will not qualify for tax relief

Who is this for?

You must meet the following criteria to be eligible:

Singapore Citizens only

Aged 55 and above as of 31st December of the assessment year, Or below 55 years old with disability status verified with the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF)

Your Retirement Account (RA) savings^ or Ordinary and Special Account (OSA) savings are less than $110,200^^

Average monthly income not more than $4,000

Annual Value of Residence not more than $21,000

Owns not more than 1 property

Your eligibility for the scheme is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year, and you do not need to apply

You can check your eligibility via your Retirement Dashboard



RA savings refers to the RA balances (excluding amounts such as interest earned), plus retirement withdrawals and CPF LIFE premium deducted. If you are below 55 or turning 55 in the assessment year and your RA has not been created at the time we assess your eligibility, your total Special Account and Ordinary Account savings will be used for the eligibility assessment instead.

^^ Current Basic Retirement Sum for 2026.

How to apply?

You do not need to apply as your eligibility is automatically assessed annually at the beginning of the year

If you are eligible, CPF Board will notify you via email or post. You can check your eligibility via your Retirement Dashboard

To receive the matching grant you need to receive cash top-ups in your RA/SA , within the year that you are eligible for MRSS

You can make a cash top-up here

Where can I find help?

View more details

CPF hotline (within Singapore): 1800-227-1188 
CPF hotline (from overseas): +65-6227-1188

To avoid waiting on the line, you may book an appointment here.

Airtime charges apply for mobile calls to 1800 service lines. Calls are free of charge only if made from regular land lines in Singapore.

Submit an enquiry

Visit your nearest branch




Scheme last updated 10 Mar 2026
SUPPORT RECOMMENDER
